authBtn.label: Sign in / sign out
authBtn.help: Create an account to save custom patches, track your sessions, and build your harmonic fingerprint over time. Your data stays yours.

noteDisplay.label: Detected note
noteDisplay.help: The note Thiri thinks you're singing right now. Updates in real time. If it's jumping around, try tightening the confidence or smoothing knobs.

freqDisplay.label: Frequency
freqDisplay.help: The raw frequency of your voice in hertz. A4 = 440 Hz. Useful for tuning reference.

pitchMeter.label: Pitch meter
pitchMeter.help: Shows how sharp or flat you are from the nearest note. Center = perfectly in tune. Left = flat. Right = sharp.

centsDisplay.label: Cents offset
centsDisplay.help: How many cents sharp (+) or flat (−) you are from the detected note. 100 cents = one semitone. ±10 cents is normal for most singers.

levelCanvas.label: Input level
levelCanvas.help: How loud your mic signal is. Green = good. Yellow = getting hot. Red = clipping. Adjust your mic distance or input gain if you're in the red.

diagnostics.label: Diagnostics
diagnostics.help: Live stats from the pitch detection engine. CONF = how sure Thiri is about the note. RMS = your volume level. GATE = whether the noise gate is open or closed. DRIFT = pitch stability. KEY = current key. CHORD = current chord from chart.

keySelect.label: Key
keySelect.help: The root note of the scale Thiri uses to build harmony. If you're singing in Bb, set this to Bb. All harmony voices will stay diatonic to this key.

modeSelect.label: Mode
modeSelect.help: The scale type. Major = bright, happy. Dorian = jazzy minor. Mixolydian = bluesy major. Blues = minor pentatonic + blue note. This changes which notes are 'legal' for the harmony voices.

voicesGroup.label: Voices
voicesGroup.help: How many harmony voices sing with you. 1 = one harmony note. 4 = full four-part harmony around your lead. More voices = richer sound but more complex.

voicingGroup.label: Voicing
voicingGroup.help: How the harmony notes are spaced. Close = tight cluster (like a barbershop quartet). Open = spread across octaves (like an orchestral arrangement). Drop-2 = second voice drops an octave (classic jazz voicing). Parallel = all voices move the same interval.

directionGroup.label: Direction
directionGroup.help: Where the harmony voices sit relative to your lead. Above = all harmony higher than you. Below = all lower. Around = voices spread above and below your note.

voiceTypeGroup.label: Voice type
voiceTypeGroup.help: The synthesis engine for harmony voices. Vocal = formant-based vocal tract modeling (choir sound, controlled by Vowel and Breath knobs). Synth = classic oscillator waveforms (sine, saw, square). FM = frequency modulation for complex metallic/bell timbres.

vowelSlider.label: Vowel
vowelSlider.help: Morphs the vocal shape of the formant voices. AH = open choir sound. EE = bright nasal. OH = rounded warm. OO = closed hum. Sweep it slowly while singing for evolving vocal textures. Only active in Vocal mode.

breathSlider.label: Breath
breathSlider.help: Adds breathy air noise to the formant voices — like a real singer's breath mixed with tone. 0% = pure tone. 50% = whispered texture. 100% = mostly air. Subtle amounts (10-20%) add realism.

voiceWave0.label: Voice 1 waveform
voiceWave0.help: The oscillator shape for this harmony voice. Sine = pure, smooth tone. Triangle = slightly brighter. Sawtooth = buzzy, rich harmonics. Square = hollow, reedy.

voiceWave1.label: Voice 2 waveform
voiceWave1.help: The oscillator shape for this harmony voice. Sine = pure, smooth tone. Triangle = slightly brighter. Sawtooth = buzzy, rich harmonics. Square = hollow, reedy.

voiceWave2.label: Voice 3 waveform
voiceWave2.help: The oscillator shape for this harmony voice. Sine = pure, smooth tone. Triangle = slightly brighter. Sawtooth = buzzy, rich harmonics. Square = hollow, reedy.

voiceWave3.label: Voice 4 waveform
voiceWave3.help: The oscillator shape for this harmony voice. Sine = pure, smooth tone. Triangle = slightly brighter. Sawtooth = buzzy, rich harmonics. Square = hollow, reedy.

voiceDetune0.label: Voice 1 detune
voiceDetune0.help: Slightly shifts this voice's pitch in cents (hundredths of a semitone). Small detune values (±5–10) add chorus-like thickness. Larger values create dissonance.

voiceDetune1.label: Voice 2 detune
voiceDetune1.help: Slightly shifts this voice's pitch in cents. Small detune values add warmth and width. Each voice detuned differently creates a natural ensemble feel.

voiceDetune2.label: Voice 3 detune
voiceDetune2.help: Slightly shifts this voice's pitch in cents. Combine with different waveforms per voice for richer textures.

voiceDetune3.label: Voice 4 detune
voiceDetune3.help: Slightly shifts this voice's pitch in cents. Try detuning voices in opposite directions (e.g. +5 and −5) for stereo width.

voiceVol0.label: Voice 1 volume
voiceVol0.help: How loud this harmony voice is in the mix. Turn individual voices up or down to shape the balance of your harmony.

voiceVol1.label: Voice 2 volume
voiceVol1.help: How loud this harmony voice is in the mix.

voiceVol2.label: Voice 3 volume
voiceVol2.help: How loud this harmony voice is in the mix.

voiceVol3.label: Voice 4 volume
voiceVol3.help: How loud this harmony voice is in the mix.

synthToggle.label: Harmony on/off
synthToggle.help: Master switch for all harmony voices. Turn off to hear just your dry voice. Turn on to hear the harmony. Useful for A/B comparison while singing.

mixSlider.label: Mix
mixSlider.help: Balance between your dry voice (left) and the harmony voices (right). Full left = only your mic. Full right = only the synth harmony. Middle = both blended.

masterSlider.label: Master volume
masterSlider.help: Overall output volume for everything — your voice and all harmony voices combined.

humanizeSlider.label: Humanize
humanizeSlider.help: Adds subtle imperfections to the harmony voices — micro-pitch wobble, slight vibrato, tiny timing variations. Makes the synth voices feel less robotic and more like real singers. 0% = perfect machine. 100% = loose and organic.

fmToggle.label: FM synthesis
fmToggle.help: Switches harmony voices from simple waveforms to FM (frequency modulation) synthesis. FM creates complex timbres — bells, brass, electric piano, metallic textures. When on, each voice's waveform becomes a carrier modulated by a second oscillator. Opens up the patch editor for deeper sound design.

gateSlider.label: Gate
gateSlider.help: Noise gate threshold. Controls how loud you need to sing before Thiri starts listening. Open = picks up everything including room noise and breaths. Tight = only responds to strong, committed notes. Start in the middle. Tighten if you're getting false triggers from background noise.

confidenceSlider.label: Confidence
confidenceSlider.help: How certain Thiri needs to be about a note before it harmonizes. Loose = responds even when it's only 60% sure what you're singing — fast but may guess wrong. Strict = waits until it's 90%+ certain — accurate but may feel slow. Trained vocalists with clear pitch can go strict. Hummers and experimenters keep it loose.

smoothingSlider.label: Smoothing
smoothingSlider.help: Averages your pitch over time to reduce jitter. None = raw tracking, follows every wobble and vibrato — responsive but the harmony may flutter. Max = heavy averaging, harmony voices are rock-solid but react slower to intentional pitch changes. Jazz singers who slide between notes keep it low. Choir singers who sustain long tones turn it up.

drumSlots.label: Drum loops
drumSlots.help: Four drum pattern slots. Click one to start playing, click it again to stop. Click a different slot while one is playing to switch patterns at the next beat boundary (Ableton-style). Patterns are synthesized — no audio files needed.

drumsVolSlider.label: Drum volume
drumsVolSlider.help: How loud the drum loops are relative to your voice and harmony. Drums are routed through the master output.

drumSlot0.label: Straight
drumSlot0.help: Classic straight-8ths rock/pop beat. Kick on 1 and 3, snare on 2 and 4, steady hi-hats. Good starting point for most songs.

drumSlot1.label: Swing
drumSlot1.help: Jazz swing feel with a shuffle on the hi-hat. Ghost notes on the snare. The backbone of jazz, blues, and swing music.

drumSlot2.label: Latin
drumSlot2.help: Son clave-based pattern with rim clicks and syncopated kick. Works for bossa nova, salsa, or Afro-Cuban grooves.

drumSlot3.label: Ballad
drumSlot3.help: Soft brush-style pattern for slow songs. Gentle kick and ghost snares. Low energy — won't overpower a quiet vocal.

chartActiveToggle.label: Drive harmony
chartActiveToggle.help: When on, the chord chart controls what key and harmony Thiri uses — the chords in your chart override the key/mode knobs. As the playhead moves through the chart, Thiri's harmony adapts to each chord. When off, the key/mode knobs control everything and the chart is just a visual reference.

chartTempo.label: BPM
chartTempo.help: Playback speed for the chord chart and drum loops in beats per minute. 60 = slow ballad. 120 = medium groove. 200 = fast bebop. Synced to the drum engine.

chartInput.label: Chart input
chartInput.help: Type your chord changes here as text. Use standard chord symbols: Cm7, F7, Bbmaj7, Edim, G7alt. Separate chords with | for bar lines. The grid below updates as you type.

chartGrid.label: Chord grid
chartGrid.help: Visual display of your chord chart. The highlighted cell is the current chord. Double-click any cell to edit it. The playhead moves left to right at the BPM you set.

chartBarsGroup.label: Bars per row
chartBarsGroup.help: How many bars to show in each row of the grid. 4 = compact, sees the whole form at once. 8 = standard lead sheet layout. 16 = extended view for longer forms.

chartLoopBtn.label: Loop
chartLoopBtn.help: When on, the chart repeats from the beginning when it reaches the end. Perfect for practicing over changes — set up your chart, hit loop, and sing.

chartNotesBtn.label: Notes
chartNotesBtn.help: Show or hide the individual chord tones inside each grid cell. When on, you'll see the notes that make up each chord (e.g. C-Eb-G-Bb for Cm7). Helpful for learning voicings and seeing what notes are available for melody.

patchesBtn.label: Patches
patchesBtn.help: Open the patch editor to design custom synth voices for your harmony. Build your own oscillator + filter + envelope + effects chain using Csound parameters. Save and load patches. Requires sign-in.

startBtn.label: Start / stop
startBtn.help: Starts or stops the mic input, pitch detection, and harmony engine. When you click start, your browser will ask for microphone permission. Use headphones to avoid feedback.

sessionsBtn.label: Sessions
sessionsBtn.help: View your practice history — which patches you used, how long you played, which keys and voicings you explored. Over time, this builds your harmonic fingerprint. Requires sign-in.

newPatchBtn.label: New patch
newPatchBtn.help: Create a blank patch to start designing a new synth voice from scratch.

patchName.label: Patch name
patchName.help: Give your patch a name you'll remember. This is how it appears in the patch selector.

patchDesc.label: Description
patchDesc.help: Optional note about what this patch sounds like or what it's for.

patchWaveform.label: Oscillator waveform
patchWaveform.help: The base waveform for this patch. Sine = pure tone. Triangle = soft buzz. Sawtooth = rich and bright. Square = hollow and reedy. FM = frequency modulation for complex timbres like bells and brass.

patchDetune.label: Oscillator detune
patchDetune.help: Global pitch offset for this patch in cents. Use to create slight detuning against other patches for thickness.

patchUnison.label: Unison voices
patchUnison.help: Stack multiple copies of the oscillator with slight pitch and timing differences. 1 = clean single voice. 4 = thick supersaw-style wall of sound.

patchAttack.label: Attack
patchAttack.help: How long it takes for the note to reach full volume after it starts. 0ms = instant hit (like a piano). 500ms+ = slow swell (like a pad or strings).

patchDecay.label: Decay
patchDecay.help: How long the volume takes to drop from the peak to the sustain level. Short decay = plucky. Long decay = smooth.

patchSustain.label: Sustain
patchSustain.help: The volume level the note holds at while you're still singing. 100% = stays at full volume. 50% = drops to half after the attack and decay.

patchRelease.label: Release
patchRelease.help: How long the note takes to fade out after you stop singing. 0ms = cuts off instantly. 2000ms+ = long tail that rings out.

patchFilterType.label: Filter type
patchFilterType.help: Shapes the tone by cutting frequencies. Lowpass = removes highs (warm, dark). Highpass = removes lows (thin, bright). Bandpass = keeps only a narrow band (nasal, focused). Notch = cuts a narrow band (hollow).

patchCutoff.label: Cutoff frequency
patchCutoff.help: Where the filter starts cutting. Low cutoff on a lowpass = dark and muffled. High cutoff = bright and open. Sweep this in real time for classic synth filter movement.

patchResonance.label: Resonance
patchResonance.help: Boosts the frequencies right at the cutoff point. 0% = gentle filter. High resonance = sharp, whistly peak. Extreme = self-oscillation (the filter sings on its own).

patchFilterEnv.label: Filter envelope
patchFilterEnv.help: How much the ADSR envelope affects the filter cutoff. 0% = filter stays static. 100% = filter sweeps dramatically with each note's attack and decay.

patchLfoWave.label: LFO waveform
patchLfoWave.help: The shape of the low-frequency oscillator that creates movement. Sine = smooth wobble. Triangle = linear sweep. Sawtooth = ramp up and snap back. Square = on/off stutter.

patchLfoRate.label: LFO rate
patchLfoRate.help: How fast the LFO oscillates. Slow (1-5 Hz) = gentle vibrato or tremolo. Medium (5-20 Hz) = rhythmic pulse. Fast (20+ Hz) = enters audio-rate territory for metallic textures.

patchLfoDepth.label: LFO depth
patchLfoDepth.help: How much the LFO affects its target. 0% = no modulation. 100% = maximum wobble. Start around 20-30% for subtle movement.

patchLfoTarget.label: LFO target
patchLfoTarget.help: What the LFO modulates. Pitch = vibrato. Filter = wah-wah sweep. Volume = tremolo. Each creates a completely different character.

patchReverbMix.label: Reverb mix
patchReverbMix.help: How much reverb (room ambience) is added. 0% = dry and close. 50% = natural room sound. 100% = cathedral wash.

patchReverbSize.label: Reverb size
patchReverbSize.help: The size of the virtual room. Small = tight reflection. Large = cavernous hall. Affects how long the reverb tail lasts.

patchDelayMix.label: Delay mix
patchDelayMix.help: How much echo is added. 0% = no echo. Higher values = more prominent repeats.

patchDelayTime.label: Delay time
patchDelayTime.help: Time between each echo repeat in milliseconds. 250ms = eighth note at 120 BPM. 500ms = quarter note. Set to match your tempo for rhythmic echoes.

patchDelayFb.label: Delay feedback
patchDelayFb.help: How many times the echo repeats. 0% = single echo. 50% = several fading repeats. 90%+ = echoes that build on themselves (careful — can run away).

patchPublic.label: Public
patchPublic.help: Share this patch with other Thiri users. When on, anyone can find and use your patch. You'll be credited as the creator.

patchDeleteBtn.label: Delete patch
patchDeleteBtn.help: Permanently remove this patch. This can't be undone.

patchSaveBtn.label: Save patch
patchSaveBtn.help: Save the current patch settings to your account. Overwrites the previous version.

_midi.label: MIDI CC Map
_midi.help: Thiri auto-detects connected MIDI controllers. Default CC mapping: CC1 (Mod Wheel) → Vowel, CC2 (Breath) → Breath, CC7 (Volume) → Master, CC11 (Expression) → Mix, CC74 → Humanize, CC71 → Gate, CC73 → Confidence, CC72 → Smoothing, CC16 → Drum Volume, CC20-23 → Drum Slots 1-4.
